assertions.title: Agent picker displays available agents for connected server

steps.expected: InlineAgentSelectorView shown when availableAgentTypes.count > 1
steps.observed: ConversationModelPickerPanel checks availableAgentTypes.count > 1 and conditionally renders InlineAgentSelectorView with all available agent types from AgentSelectionStore

steps.expected: ForEach over agentTypes, each showing icon, name, transport label, and checkmark for selected
steps.observed: InlineAgentSelectorView uses ForEach(agentTypes) with AgentBadgeView (icon + label), displayName, transportLabel, and checkmark when agentType == selectedAgentType

steps.expected: Agent types come from Rust FFI discovery data, not hardcoded
steps.observed: AgentTypes populated from DiscoveredServer.agentTypes which comes from Rust FFI AppDiscoveredServer.agent_types field. DiscoveryView.discoveryAgentTypes() calls AgentSelectionStore.shared.updateAgentTypes(). Default is [.codex] when no discovery data available.

steps.expected: All required variants: Codex, PiAcp, PiNative, DroidAcp, DroidNative, GenericAcp
steps.observed: AgentType is #[derive(uniffi::Enum)] with exactly these 6 variants. Generated Swift bindings in codex_mobile_client.swift confirm the same variants.

steps.action: Verify accent color highlights selected agent
steps.expected: Selected agent shown with checkmark in LitterTheme.accent color (#00FF9C)
steps.observed: Image(systemName: 'checkmark').foregroundColor(LitterTheme.accent) when agentType == selectedAgentType

steps.action: Verify persistence of selection via AgentSelectionStore
steps.expected: Selected agent persisted to UserDefaults on change
steps.observed: ConversationModelPickerPanel.onChange(of: selectedAgentType) calls AgentSelectionStore.shared.setSelectedAgentType(). Store writes persistentKey to UserDefaults under 'litter.selectedAgent.<serverId>' key.

assertions.title: Agent picker filters to transports available on active connection

steps.action: Search iOS codebase for transport filtering/graying logic
steps.expected: Agents whose detected_transports don't intersect server's available transports shown grayed out with 'Transport unavailable' subtitle
steps.observed: NO transport filtering or graying logic found in InlineAgentSelectorView, AgentPickerSheet, or any iOS view. All agents from AgentSelectionStore are listed as-is with full selection capability. No code checks detected_transports intersection with available server transports. No grayed-out/disabled styling based on transport compatibility.

steps.action: Check Rust AgentInfo.detected_transports usage in iOS
steps.expected: iOS reads AgentInfo.detectedTransports and filters display accordingly
steps.observed: AgentInfo is a UniFFI record with detectedTransports field, but iOS agent picker uses AgentType enum directly (not AgentInfo records). The InlineAgentSelectorView takes [AgentType] not [AgentInfo]. The detected_transports field is never read by any iOS view for filtering purposes.

steps.action: Check for 'Transport unavailable' text in codebase
steps.expected: String 'Transport unavailable' or equivalent appears in agent picker
steps.observed: No match for 'Transport unavailable', 'grayed', or 'disabled.*transport' patterns in iOS source.

assertions.issues: Transport filtering is not implemented in the iOS agent picker. The contract requires agents with incompatible transports to appear grayed out with 'Transport unavailable' subtitle. The Rust AgentInfo.detected_transports field exists but is not consumed by iOS views. All agents are shown as fully selectable regardless of transport compatibility.

assertions.title: Agent type badge renders correct icon and color per AgentType variant

steps.expected: Codex→terminal.fill, Pi→brain, Droid→robot, GenericAcp→gearshape.fill
steps.observed: Exact mapping: .codex→'terminal.fill', .piAcp/.piNative→'brain', .droidAcp/.droidNative→'robot', .genericAcp→'gearshape.fill'. Matches contract specification.

steps.expected: Codex→green, Pi→purple, Droid→orange, GenericAcp→gray
steps.observed: Exact mapping: .codex→LitterTheme.accent (green #00FF9C), .piAcp/.piNative→Color.purple, .droidAcp/.droidNative→Color.orange, .genericAcp→Color.gray. Matches contract specification.

steps.expected: Badge renders icon with tint color in compact form
steps.observed: AgentBadgeView: Image(systemName: agentType.icon) + optional displayName text, both with agentType.tintColor. AgentTypePill: icon + displayName with tintColor, pill background using tintColor.opacity(0.15) and Capsule shape.

steps.action: Verify badge used in SessionsScreen for session rows
steps.expected: Session rows show agent badge for non-Codex agents
steps.observed: SessionsScreen.agentBadgeType(for:) returns the selected agent type when non-Codex, then renders AgentTypePill(agentType:). Badge updates on store snapshot changes.

steps.expected: Header shows agent pill when multi-agent or non-Codex
steps.observed: HeaderView.showAgentBadge returns true when availableAgentTypes.count > 1 || currentAgentType != .codex. When true, renders AgentTypePill(agentType: currentAgentType).

steps.action: Verify badge used in DiscoveryView server cards
steps.expected: Discovery shows detected agents per server
steps.observed: DiscoveryView.serverRow shows ScrollView with ForEach(detectedAgentTypes) { AgentBadgeView(agentType:, size: 10, showsLabel: true) } when detectedAgentTypes.count > 1 or first != .codex.

assertions.title: Connection flow: connect → select agent → start session

steps.expected: When server has >1 agent types and no selection made, agent picker sheet presented
steps.observed: handleTapAsync checks: let needsPicker = server.agentTypes.count > 1 && AgentSelectionStore.shared.selectedAgentType(for: server.id) == nil. If needsPicker, sets agentPickerServer = server and agentPickerSelection = nil.

steps.expected: AgentPickerSheet shown with server's agent types
steps.observed: .sheet(item: $agentPickerServer) { server in AgentPickerSheet(agentTypes: server.agentTypes, selectedAgentType: $agentPickerSelection) }

steps.expected: Selecting agent persists selection and continues connection flow
steps.observed: AgentPickerSheet button sets selectedAgentType = agentType and calls dismiss(). DiscoveryView.onChange(of: agentPickerSelection) calls AgentSelectionStore.shared.setSelectedAgentType(selected, for: server.id), then proceeds with connection via Task { await proceedWithConnection(server) }.

steps.action: Verify auto-select when only one agent available
steps.expected: Picker skipped when server has only one agent type
steps.observed: handleTapAsync: if server.agentTypes.count == 1 { AgentSelectionStore.shared.setSelectedAgentType(server.agentTypes.first, for: server.id) }. No agentPickerServer set, so sheet never shown. Connection proceeds directly.

steps.action: Verify already-connected server can still show picker
steps.expected: If connected but no agent selected, picker shown on tap
steps.observed: When server is already connected: let needsPicker = server.agentTypes.count > 1 && AgentSelectionStore.shared.selectedAgentType(for: server.id) == nil. If needsPicker, shows agentPickerServer sheet.

steps.expected: After agent selection, normal connection logic runs
steps.observed: proceedWithConnection checks if already connected (navigates), otherwise checks requiresConnectionChoice, hasCodexServer, or canConnectViaSSH to continue the standard connection flow.

assertions.title: Agent-specific controls appear and disappear correctly on agent switch

steps.expected: Pi thinking level shown when activeAgentType.isPi, Droid autonomy shown when activeAgentType.isDroid, neither for Codex
steps.observed: InlineModelSelectorView.body has conditional: if activeAgentType.isPi { PiThinkingLevelPicker } else if activeAgentType.isDroid { DroidAutonomyControl }. No else branch for Codex, meaning no extra controls shown.

steps.action: Inspect AgentType.isPi and isDroid computed properties
steps.expected: isPi true for .piAcp and .piNative; isDroid true for .droidAcp and .droidNative
steps.observed: AgentBadgeView.swift: isPi returns true for .piAcp, .piNative. isDroid returns true for .droidAcp, .droidNative. All other cases return false.

steps.expected: Segmented picker with Quick/Balanced/Deep options
steps.observed: PiThinkingLevelPicker with 3 levels: ('Quick', 'off'), ('Balanced', 'medium'), ('Deep', 'high'). Shows brain icon in purple. Active level gets purple background, inactive gets surfaceLight.

steps.expected: Slider/buttons with Cautious/Normal/Aggressive options
steps.observed: DroidAutonomyControl with 3 levels: ('Cautious', 'suggest'), ('Normal', 'normal'), ('Aggressive', 'full'). Shows robot icon in orange. Active level gets orange background.

steps.action: Verify activeAgentType flows from agent selection
steps.expected: Changing agent in InlineAgentSelectorView updates activeAgentType in InlineModelSelectorView
steps.observed: ConversationModelPickerPanel passes selectedAgentType as activeAgentType to InlineModelSelectorView. onChange(of: selectedAgentType) persists to AgentSelectionStore. The conditional rendering of PiThinkingLevelPicker vs DroidAutonomyControl vs nothing responds to activeAgentType changes immediately.

steps.action: Verify no stale controls after agent switch
steps.expected: Switching from Pi to Codex removes thinking level; switching from Droid to Pi removes autonomy and shows thinking level
steps.observed: The if/else if chain ensures only ONE control is visible at a time: isPi→PiThinkingLevelPicker, isDroid→DroidAutonomyControl, else→nothing. SwiftUI re-renders on activeAgentType change, so switching immediately shows/hides the correct control.

assertions.title: Agent picker persists across app restarts

steps.expected: Selected agent type persisted to UserDefaults
steps.observed: AgentSelectionStore.setSelectedAgentType writes to UserDefaults.standard under key 'litter.selectedAgent.<serverId>' with agentType.persistentKey string value.

steps.action: Inspect AgentSelectionStore initialization — loading persisted data
steps.expected: On init, AgentSelectionStore reads persisted selection from UserDefaults
steps.observed: selectedAgentType(for:) reads from UserDefaults.standard.string(forKey:). Returns AgentType.fromPersistentKey(key) which maps string back to AgentType enum variant.

steps.expected: UserDefaults.standard persists across app launches
steps.observed: UserDefaults is Apple's standard persistence mechanism. Data written to UserDefaults.standard survives app termination and relaunch. The agent selection key 'litter.selectedAgent.<serverId>' is a standard string key that persists.

steps.action: Verify agent types cache also persisted
steps.expected: Discovered agent types for servers also survive restarts
steps.observed: AgentSelectionStore persists serverAgentTypesCache to UserDefaults under 'litter.serverAgentTypes' key as JSON-encoded [String: [String]] dictionary mapping server IDs to arrays of persistentKey strings. On init, this cache is loaded and decoded.

steps.action: Verify HeaderView loads persisted selection on appear
steps.expected: HeaderView shows previously selected agent after restart
steps.observed: ConversationModelPickerPanel.task(id: thread.key) calls AgentSelectionStore.shared.effectiveAgentType() which checks selectedAgentType(for:) from UserDefaults first. The selectedAgentType state is initialized from this persisted value.

steps.expected: AgentType has Codable conformance for serialization
steps.observed: AgentPickerView.swift adds @retroactive Codable conformance via a rawValueMap mapping each variant to a stable string key (codex, piAcp, piNative, droidAcp, droidNative, genericAcp). encode/decode use singleValueContainer with the persistentKey string.

frictions.description: VAL-UI-002 requires testing agent grayed-out behavior for transport-incompatible agents, but the codebase doesn't use AgentInfo records in the picker — it uses AgentType enums directly. The AgentInfo.detected_transports field exists in Rust but isn't consumed by iOS views for filtering.
frictions.resolution: This is an implementation gap, not a testing friction. The feature is simply not implemented.

summary: Tested 6 assertions: 5 passed, 1 failed (VAL-UI-002: transport filtering not implemented). Agent types come from Rust FFI (not hardcoded), badges map correctly to icons/colors, connection flow wires agent picker sheet properly, agent-specific controls (Pi thinking level, Droid autonomy) appear/disappear correctly based on activeAgentType, and agent selection persists to UserDefaults across app restarts.
